evidence: sync-check.sh surface-drift check detects source vs deployed-copy mismatch only. When TDD subagent commits both source and deployed copy together (via build.sh), drift is zero and the check passes silently. Claude in-memory plugin cache still holds old skill content with no user notification. Root cause: no mechanism compared plugin dirs against session branch-point git baseline.
proposed_handoff: Implemented: add git merge-base branch-point detection to sync-check.sh; emit green [rabbit] systemMessage listing changed files and instructing /reload-plugins when any .claude/skills/, .claude/commands/, or .claude/agents/ files changed since branch-point. Single-JSON-per-invocation invariant enforced via _json_emitted flag.
